# Questions in Livestream 58

Live Stream #58: Is Like The Luckiest Number Ever

Time links open the YouTube video at the relevant timestamp. The links use HTML anchors so GitHub can render them with `target="_blank"`.

| Time | Question | Short answer / answer direction |
|---:|---|---|
| <a href="https://youtu.be/iMKb3EZ1uw0?t=436" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">7:16</a> | What makes Jesus different from religious figures such as Muhammad, Buddha, Joseph Smith, Abraham, or Moses? | He said Jesus is different because he claimed to be God incarnate and supported that claim through his life, miracles, death, and resurrection. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/iMKb3EZ1uw0?t=746" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">12:26</a> | What happened to the old Spotify or podcast channel? | He said it was not really a Spotify channel but an Anchor FM feed, and they stopped maintaining it after a bad platform experience. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/iMKb3EZ1uw0?t=1051" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">17:31</a> | Do Exodus 21 and Numbers 5 really refer to miscarriage? | He argued that Exodus 21 is about premature childbirth with no further injury, while Numbers 5 concerns a suspected adultery oath and sterility, not miscarriage. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/iMKb3EZ1uw0?t=1520" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">25:20</a> | Were ancient Egyptians prudish or hedonistic about sex and marriage? | Neither prudish nor especially restrained. He described them as sexually liberal, with frequent infidelity and comparatively relaxed norms. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/iMKb3EZ1uw0?t=1679" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">27:59</a> | What measuring systems did Israel use before and after the Exodus? | He said Israel shared common Near Eastern measures such as the cubit and span, while Egyptian and Israelite weight systems diverged more clearly in units like the deben and shekel. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/iMKb3EZ1uw0?t=1940" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">32:20</a> | In 2 Samuel 12:23, is David saying only that he will join his dead child in the grave, or something more hopeful? | He thought the verse points at least toward reunion after death, even if the text does not describe that afterlife in detail. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/iMKb3EZ1uw0?t=2228" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">37:08</a> | What is the date of the real Book of Jasher, and why was it not preserved? | He said we do not know its date, and its loss is not unusual because most ancient works were never copied enough to survive. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/iMKb3EZ1uw0?t=2835" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">47:15</a> | Does the death of David and Bathsheba's first son contradict Deuteronomy 24:16? | He said no, because Deuteronomy limits human courts, while David's case involved extraordinary divine judgment on an otherwise untouchable king. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/iMKb3EZ1uw0?t=3436" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">57:16</a> | What are the differences between Egyptians and Nubians? | He distinguished them geographically, ethnographically, politically, and culturally, noting Egyptian rule over Nubia, the later Nubian 25th Dynasty, and Meroitic culture. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/iMKb3EZ1uw0?t=4003" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:06:43</a> | What does he think of Jordan Peterson's *Psychological Significance of the Bible* series? | He credited Peterson as a positive public intellectual but judged the biblical interpretation itself as weak and too controlled by a Jungian lens. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/iMKb3EZ1uw0?t=4426" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:13:46</a> | How could Pharaoh's magicians imitate the plague of frogs? | He said the text does not explain how, but Egyptian priests used ritual magic, sleight of hand, and possibly practical means such as irritants. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/iMKb3EZ1uw0?t=4710" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:18:30</a> | What geological evidence should support a global flood? | He mentioned fossils in high places and rapid sedimentation, but left the larger global, regional, or semi-global flood question open. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/iMKb3EZ1uw0?t=5140" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:25:40</a> | Does rejecting or declining to endorse evolutionary theory damage his credibility as an Egyptologist? | He said his credibility does not rest on endorsing evolution, and that he has studied the subject but chooses not to take a dogmatic position. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/iMKb3EZ1uw0?t=5506" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:31:46</a> | Why do some people distrust Paul, and is that reversible? | He said both legalists and antinomians can dislike Paul's balance of grace and good works; reversing it requires returning to the gospel itself. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/iMKb3EZ1uw0?t=5941" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:39:01</a> | Is there ancient extra-biblical literature from non-Israelite cultures that discusses or venerates the Israelite God? | He said Yahweh is not clearly discussed that way, but names and deities such as El or El Elyon appear in wider Northwest Semitic evidence. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/iMKb3EZ1uw0?t=6195" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:43:15</a> | Who or what is the restrainer, the *katechon*, in 2 Thessalonians 2:6-7? | He said the text is not explicit, but the usual theological interpretation is that the restrainer is the Holy Spirit. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/iMKb3EZ1uw0?t=6634" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:50:34</a> | What sparked his interest in Egyptology? | He traced it to childhood exposure to King Tut television specials, the *Doctor Who* episode "Pyramids of Mars," library books, and school projects. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/iMKb3EZ1uw0?t=6907" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:55:07</a> | Why do some Old Kingdom royal statues lack a uraeus on the headdress? | He said the pattern is inconsistent and probably partly stylistic; some Old Kingdom statues do have a uraeus, so there is no simple universal rule. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/iMKb3EZ1uw0?t=7361" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:02:41</a> | What is the prince of Persia in Daniel 10:13, and how was he stronger than Gabriel? | He understood the passage as a battle with regional spiritual powers; the issue was not superior strength but that Gabriel faced multiple opposing powers. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/iMKb3EZ1uw0?t=7731" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:08:51</a> | If Genesis 6 is not about angels, do Peter and Jude create an inerrancy problem by referring to fallen angels? | He said Peter and Jude do not explicitly tie fallen angels to Genesis 6, and multiple interpretive levels could also avoid an inerrancy problem. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/iMKb3EZ1uw0?t=8075" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:14:35</a> | How would he explain Brueggemann's claim that Hebrew is imprecise and suggestive rather than conclusive? | He agreed that Hebrew has broad lexical ranges and imprecise relationships between words, so careful exegesis must consider context and alternatives. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/iMKb3EZ1uw0?t=8580" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:23:00</a> | If he had complete creative control over an Exodus movie, what controversial choices would he make? | He would emphasize period accuracy, avoid pyramid backdrops, show the inundation cycle behind the plagues, and portray clothing, setting, and production design differently. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/iMKb3EZ1uw0?t=8948" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:29:08</a> | Why did Spurgeon, Edwards, Gill, Wesley, and others think Michael was Jesus? | He said they were reading Daniel 12:1 christologically, but he prefers seeing Michael as the primary referent and as a type of Christ rather than Christ himself. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/iMKb3EZ1uw0?t=9297" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:34:57</a> | Have his studies led him to the Hypsistarians, and what does he know about them? | He said he has not studied them, has not run across them, and does not know about them. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/iMKb3EZ1uw0?t=9361" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:36:01</a> | If a math textbook had one false page, would he throw away the whole textbook and give up on math? | No. He used the analogy to distinguish original authorship from copying or typesetting errors, and said inerrancy concerns the autographs rather than later copies. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/iMKb3EZ1uw0?t=9634" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:40:34</a> | Would he throw away a Rohl or Down book on Egyptian chronology? | He was sharply critical of both, saying Rohl's views keep changing and Down's work is poor, though he might still buy a cheap used copy for engagement. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/iMKb3EZ1uw0?t=9774" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:42:54</a> | Are there good resources on Luwian language and culture? | He recommended Annick Payne's *Hieroglyphic Luwian: An Introduction with Original Texts* for the language. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/iMKb3EZ1uw0?t=9880" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:44:40</a> | If pyramids were tombs, why did Egypt also have the Valley of the Kings, and is there evidence for another use? | He said pyramids were royal tombs and necropolis centers; the Valley of the Kings developed because rock-cut tombs were cheaper and faster, not because pyramids had another use. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/iMKb3EZ1uw0?t=10240" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:50:40</a> | Besides Exodus, what Bible stories would he like to see filmed and consult on? | He mentioned David, the conquest, and especially Joseph, because those stories would allow historically rich settings, clothing, architecture, and Egyptian detail. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/iMKb3EZ1uw0?t=10545" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:55:45</a> | What does it mean that an evil spirit from the Lord tormented Saul in 1 Samuel 16? | He read it as divine permission rather than God directly committing evil: God removed protection and allowed a hostile spirit to act. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/iMKb3EZ1uw0?t=11339" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:08:59</a> | What harm has Hollywood done to biblical understanding? | He said sensationalized films distort the text, and the larger damage grows when churches then use those distortions in place of serious biblical teaching. |
